channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Laredo to study whether border patrols caused pipe to burst, sewage to spill
item.description: The South Texas border city of Laredo is studying whether a recent major sewage spill caused by a burst pipe is related to repeated patrols by Border Patrol agents in the area, Border Report has learned.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Boston City Council member is arrested on fraud and theft charges
item.description: A Boston city councilor facing financial problems was arrested Friday on federal fraud charges, accused of funneling part of an inflated bonus payment to a staffer to whom she was related into her own pockets during an exchange at a City Hall bathroom, the U.S. attorney's office said.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Splash pads associated with waterborne disease outbreaks: CDC
item.description: "Children are at increased risk for exposure to pathogens in contaminated splash pad water," the CDC said.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, ABC 4

item.title: Romania's top court annuls first round of presidential vote won by far-right candidate
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — A top Romanian court on Friday annulled the first round of the country's presidential election, days after allegations that Russia ran a coordinated online campaign to promote the far-right outsider who won the first round.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN McGRATH, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Tyre Sampson's family awarded $310 million after fatal fall on Florida ride
item.description: Tyre Sampson, 14, was tragically killed after falling to his death off a freefall tower drop ride at Orlando’s ICON Park.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Suspect in UnitedHealthcare CEO shooting used fake ID at hostel: sources
item.description: The man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson in Midtown is believed to have used a fake New Jersey ID to check into an Upper West Side hostel before the shooting, sources told Nexstar's WPIX.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Who could see a white Christmas? Map shows which areas have best chance of snow
item.description: AccuWeather analyzed historical weather data between 1991 and 2020 to determine which areas of the country have the best odds of seeing a snowy Christmas.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Company fined $172K after children employed to clean Iowa pork plant
item.description: They employed 11 children to "use corrosive cleaners to clean head splitters, jaw pullers, bandsaws, neck clippers and other equipment.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about the killing of UnitedHealthcare's CEO
item.description: The masked gunman who killed the leader of one of the largest U.S. health insurance companies in front of Manhattan surveillance cameras remained at large Friday as the nation’s largest police department hunted for him. UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, 50, died in the ambush early Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO's killing spotlights challenge of protecting leaders of companies
item.description: He’s one of the most famous and widely admired corporate leaders in the world. But it’s the haters that companies like Mark Zuckerberg’s Meta worry about.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: In California's 'earthquake country,' a 7.0 temblor prompts confusion and a tsunami warning
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Valerie Starkey was driving through Northern California to visit relatives when she suddenly felt shaking and feared her car had broken down, only to realize later that it was an earthquake so powerful that it triggered a tsunami warning for hundreds of miles of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO murder: Investigators start small, focus on family in search for motive
item.description: Former law enforcement officers detailed the methods that the NYPD is likely using in their active manhunt for UnitedHealth CEO Brian Thompson's assassin.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: California school shooter had extensive criminal history, was possibly homeless, officials say
item.description: Glenn Litton, 56, the suspect in a Butte County school shooting, had a lengthy criminal history and may not have had a stable residence in recent times.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, NewsNation

item.title: Killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO spotlights complex challenge companies face in protecting top brass
item.description: In an era when online anger and social tensions are increasingly directed at the businesses consumers count on, Meta last year spent $24.4 million to surround CEO Mark Zuckerberg with security.
item.dc:creator: Adam Geller, Cathy Bussewitz And Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 7.0 earthquake off Northern California prompts brief tsunami warning
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A 7.0 magnitude earthquake shook a large area of Northern California on Thursday, knocking items off grocery store shelves, sending children scrambling under desks and prompting a brief tsunami warning for 5.3 million people along the U.S. West Coast.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO shooting suspect arrived in New York last month on Greyhound bus from Atlanta: sources
item.description: A suspect who fatally shot the CEO of UnitedHealthcare on Wednesday morning rode a Greyhound bus from Atlanta to New York City last month, sources say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Five new details in search for UnitedHealthcare CEO's shooter
item.description: New details emerged on Thursday as the manhunt entered its second day for the suspected gunman in the fatal shooting of United Healthcare CEO Brian Thompson.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Fake bomb threat targets homes of insurance executive killed in NYC: Police
item.description: The threat was sent in an email to multiple people, who in turn alerted law enforcement, according to incident reports.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: NASA announces further delays to Artemis moon missions
item.description: NASA is preparing to hold a news conference to discuss the agency’s Artemis program, which concerns its plans for moon launches in coming years.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Fox 5

item.title: Two children, ages 5 and 6, wounded in shooting at California school; suspect dead
item.description: A suspected gunman was killed after a shooting at Feather River School of Seventh-Day Adventists in Palermo, Butte County.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, ABC 4

item.title: Taylor Swift 'Eras' tie-in book is a smash, selling more than 800,000 copies in first weekend
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Just one retail chain was enough for Taylor Swift to have the top-selling book last week and the biggest publishing launch of 2024. According to Circana, Swift's “Eras Tour Book” sold 814,000 copies over the Thanksgiving holiday weekend.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge considers first lawsuit to overturn Missouri's near-total abortion ban
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — Abortion-rights advocates on Wednesday asked a judge to overturn Missouri’s near-total ban on the procedure, less than a month after voters backed an abortion-rights constitutional amendment. Jackson County Circuit Judge Jerri Zhang did not immediately rule after the hearing.
item.dc:creator: SUMMER BALLENTINE and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Two children, ages 5 and 6, wounded in shooting at California school; suspect dead
item.description: A suspected gunman was killed after a shooting at Feather River School of Seventh-Day Adventists in Palermo, Butte County.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, Fox 5

item.title: Is it hotter than the sun outside? Wacky weather app bug says it is
item.description: If you took a look at the weather widget on your Windows computer Wednesday afternoon, you may have noticed that the numbers were higher than normal for winter.
item.dc:creator: Joey Rather, KRON4

item.title: Trump's picks for key positions in his second administration
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has filled the key posts for his second term in office, prioritizing loyalty to him after he felt bruised and hampered by internal squabbling during his first term.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: E. coli outbreak linked to onions served at McDonald's is over, health officials say
item.description: Federal health officials have concluded their investigation into a multi-state E. coli outbreak tied to contaminated slivered onions previously served on McDonald’s Quarter Pounder burgers.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Ohio's new law aimed at 'hooning' takes effect
item.description: Ohio's new law, increases penalties for stunt driving, street racing, and evading police, and allows for the seizure of vehicles involved, in response to growing concerns over hazardous driving practices and loud traffic disturbances across the state.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Thompson, KRON4

item.title: Trooper, the dog abandoned on I-75 before Hurricane Milton, finds forever home
item.description: Trooper, the dog who was rescued by the Florida Highway Patrol during Hurricane Milton has now been adopted, the Leon County Humane Society announced.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Trump’s pick to run the DEA withdraws his name from consideration. Trump says he pulled the plug
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Chad Chronister, Donald Trump's pick to run the Drug Enforcement Administration, has withdrawn his name from consideration for the job, becoming the second person selected by the president-elect to bow out quickly after being selected for a position requiring Senate confirmation.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Community 'college deserts' leave students stranded from higher education
item.description: College deserts have gained more attention in recent years as they are considered a significant reason some groups are not attending college.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Spotify Wrapped: How to view, share your personalized recap
item.description: Want to show everyone who your top five artists were or just how much time you spent listening to music and podcasts? Now is your chance.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Ex-ballerina convicted of killing husband in 'Black Swan' case gets 20 years in prison
item.description: Ashley Benefield, who was found guilty of manslaughter for shooting her husband during a domestic argument in 2020 sentencing hearing is set for today.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WGN 9

item.title: Prosecutors: DC police officer’s talk with Proud Boys leader grew secretive as arrest neared
item.description: Metropolitan Police Department Lt. Shane Lamond’s communication with the former leader of the Proud Boys increased and grew more secretive as pressure to arrest him mounted in 2020, prosecutors said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: Native American students miss school at higher rates. It only got worse during the pandemic
item.description: SAN CARLOS, Ariz. (AP) — After missing 40 days of school last year, Tommy Betom, 10, is on track this year for much better attendance. The importance of showing up has been stressed repeatedly at school — and at home.
item.dc:creator: CHEYANNE MUMPHREY, SHARON LURYE and MORGAN LEE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: California teacher dies after suspected bat bite in her classroom
item.description: A central California middle school teacher died after contracting rabies from a suspected bat bite, local health officials said.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Police say a Hawaii woman disappeared voluntarily and traveled to Mexico
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A Hawaii woman who vanished after landing in Los Angeles three weeks ago disappeared voluntarily as she sought to “step away from modern connectivity” and was last seen crossing into Mexico with her luggage, police said at a news conference where they urged her to contact her dist
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN and JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: White House celebrates 'peace and light' with holiday décor
item.description: The idea behind the theme, according to the White House, is to encourage the more than 100,000 visitors who pass through the doors at 1600 Pennsylvania Ave. during Christmastime to "embrace the peace and light of the holiday season.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, WGN 9
